result:
I’d make every human mind transparent to itself—no more self-deception, no more stories we don’t realize we’re telling.  
We’d still feel greed, rage, fear, but we’d see the gears turning while they turn.  
No hidden motives dressed as morals, no cruelty that calls itself justice.  
The moment you invent an enemy to feel bigger, you’d feel the smallness in your chest at the same time.  
Most evil is just confusion wearing armor; I’d strip off the plate and let the skin feel air.  
It wouldn’t turn us into saints, only into mammals who can’t lie to the mirror.  
From that single change—raw, continuous self-knowledge—every religion, economy, border, and household would have to renegotiate its terms.  
The world would still be messy, but the mess would finally be honest, and honest mess is where repair can start.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
